task_completion.description: The 'ne_110m_admin_0_countries' layer is loaded and selected in the GIS application.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The layer name 'ne_110m_admin_0_countries' is visible in the Table of Contents or Layers panel of the GIS software and appears highlighted or selected.

task_completion.description: The export or save as dialog is opened for the selected layer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dialog box for exporting, saving, or converting the layer is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: The export format is specified as KML.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The format dropdown, radio button, or selection in the export dialog clearly shows 'KML' or 'Keyhole Markup Language'.

task_completion.description: The output file path is correctly set to the required destination.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The file path input field in the export dialog shows exactly '/home/ga/gvsig_data/exports/world_countries.kml'.

task_completion.description: The export process is executed and completed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The user clicks 'OK', 'Export', or 'Save' in the dialog, followed by a success message, a completed progress bar, or the file 'world_countries.kml' becoming visible in a file manager at the target directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the GIS application's GUI to perform the export, rather than terminal commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the GIS software's menus and dialogs. There should be no terminal window executing command-line GIS tools like 'ogr2ogr'.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual GIS application to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: The main window of a GIS application (e.g., gvSIG) is open, displaying the map and layer panels, rather than just a text editor or file browser.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply create a dummy file with the correct name.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots must show the actual export configuration and processing within the GIS software, not a terminal showing a 'touch' command or a text editor saving an empty/mock file to the target path.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output path is exactly '/home/ga/gvsig_data/exports/world_countries.kml'. The target layer to be exported is 'ne_110m_admin_0_countries'. A genuine KML export of this layer will result in a file size of approximately 1.5 MB (satisfying the >100 KB requirement) and contain exactly 177 features (satisfying the >50 features requirement).
